overview: Implement the Marrow toolchain described in docs/discription.md. Phase 1 (MAR-001–039) delivered core runtime and editor. Phase 2 (MAR-040–066) fixed Spine compliance bugs and added GL rendering. Phase 3 (MAR-067+) targets production readiness: multi-backend rendering, game engine integration, Spine import, atlas packing, performance, artist workflow, and documentation.
qualityGates: Treat docs/discription.md as the feature and architecture source of truth unless the active story explicitly updates it.
Keep each story small enough for one Ralph iteration and preserve the documented dependency order: renderer/runtime validation before advanced editor work.
When a story changes file formats, update checked-in sample assets and any matching operational docs in the same iteration.
Every completed story must leave the repository in a clean git state with build, test, or inspection commands recorded in AGENTS.md and the Ralph progress log.
Prefer runtime-verifiable slices over placeholder scaffolding; if a feature cannot be fully validated yet, add the smallest executable test harness that proves the implemented slice.
Bug-fix stories must include a regression test that would have caught the original defect, added to marrow_fixture_smoke or a dedicated test target.

stories.title: Bootstrap the Marrow C++ workspace
stories.description: Create the initial repository layout, CMake entrypoint, source and include directories, and one compilable placeholder target so the project can evolve into a runtime and editor codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: A top-level CMakeLists.txt exists and configures successfully.
Runtime and editor-oriented source/include directories exist with at least one compilable translation unit.
AGENTS.md documents the configure and build commands.

stories.title: Create renderer and sample-app scaffolding
stories.description: Add a minimal render-facing module and demo application shell that can host atlas rendering and dynamic mesh work before the full runtime is implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: A renderer module target exists in the build.
A small sample or demo executable can be built and launched.
The sample executable is the documented validation target for rendering-related stories.

stories.title: Check in canonical .mskl and .matl fixtures
stories.description: Convert the format examples in docs/discription.md into checked-in fixture assets that cover skeleton metadata, bones, slots, animations, and atlas metadata for repeatable tests.
acceptanceCriteria: A sample .mskl file exists under an examples or assets directory.
A sample .matl file exists and references plausible atlas regions used by the sample skeleton.
The repository documents how the fixtures map back to docs/discription.md.

stories.title: Implement JSON parsing infrastructure
stories.description: Add the JSON loading layer used by Marrow runtime data so fixture assets can be loaded from disk with validation and clear errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime code can read the sample .mskl and .matl files from disk.
Parse failures produce actionable error output instead of silent failure.
A smoke-test command proves both fixture files parse successfully.

stories.title: Model SkeletonData and Skeleton core types
stories.description: Implement the runtime-side static and instance data containers from the architecture section, separating immutable imported data from per-instance animation state.
acceptanceCriteria: SkeletonData and Skeleton types exist with clear ownership boundaries.
Parsed fixture data can populate SkeletonData and spawn a Skeleton instance.
The code distinguishes setup data from mutable instance pose state.

stories.title: Implement setup pose and bone hierarchy evaluation
stories.description: Add the first world-transform pass so the runtime can resolve parent-child bone transforms and reset a skeleton to setup pose.
acceptanceCriteria: Skeleton can reset to setup pose without reparsing assets.
Parent-child world transforms are computed for the sample bone chain.
A verification command or test asserts at least one expected world transform result.

stories.title: Implement AtlasLoader and region lookup
stories.description: Create the atlas metadata loader and lookup API described in the architecture so runtime attachments can resolve named regions through .matl data.
acceptanceCriteria: AtlasLoader parses the sample .matl metadata into typed structures.
Region lookup by attachment or region name works for the fixture atlas.
The atlas loader is wired into the runtime sample or tests.

stories.title: Render setup-pose region attachments
stories.description: Connect the renderer scaffold, atlas metadata, and skeleton world transforms so the sample skeleton can render static region attachments in setup pose.
acceptanceCriteria: The sample app can render region attachments using atlas lookups.
Rendered attachment placement follows computed world transforms.
The validation path for rendering is documented in AGENTS.md.

stories.title: Implement interpolation primitives
stories.description: Add runtime support for linear, stepped, and cubic bezier interpolation as described in the format spec so animation timelines can evaluate correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Linear, stepped, and bezier interpolation evaluators exist in runtime code.
Fixture-driven tests cover the documented curve encodings.
Timeline code can consume the interpolation helpers without ad hoc branching.

stories.title: Implement bone transform timelines
stories.description: Add runtime evaluation for rotate, translate, scale, and shear timelines so animations can change bone transforms over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Animation data can drive bone rotation and translation from fixture data.
Scale and shear timeline support is represented in runtime structures and evaluation code.
A verification command or test confirms timeline sampling changes the sample pose over time.

stories.title: Implement slot timelines
stories.description: Add attachment swap, slot color or alpha, and draw-order timeline support so runtime playback can affect slot-driven presentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Slot attachment changes over time are supported by the runtime.
Slot color or alpha timelines are represented and evaluated.
Draw-order changes can reorder sample slots during playback.

stories.title: Implement event timelines and callbacks
stories.description: Add named animation events and runtime callback plumbing so gameplay hooks like footsteps and hit effects can be triggered from animation playback.
acceptanceCriteria: Event definitions and per-animation event keys can be parsed from fixture data.
Runtime playback emits callbacks when event keys are reached.
A smoke test or sample output proves event data values are surfaced correctly.

stories.title: Implement AnimationState tracks and mixing
stories.description: Build the track-based AnimationState layer from the architecture section, including queueing, crossfade mixing, empty animations, and loop completion callbacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Multiple tracks can play or queue animations independently.
Mix durations and empty-animation fade-out behavior are supported.
Start, interrupt, complete, end, dispose, and event-style callbacks are wired through the animation state API.

stories.title: Implement skin system and linked meshes
stories.description: Add runtime support for named skins, skin composition, linked mesh inheritance, and skin-specific attachments so character variants can share skeleton structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Skins can be switched at runtime on the sample skeleton.
Linked mesh metadata can reference parent mesh data without duplication.
The runtime exposes a small API for whole-skin swap and partial skin composition.

stories.title: Implement weighted mesh attachments
stories.description: Add mesh attachment support with per-vertex bone weights so joints can deform smoothly beyond rigid region attachments.
acceptanceCriteria: Mesh attachment structures store vertices, triangles, UVs, and weights.
The runtime can evaluate weighted vertex transforms for a sample mesh.
A sample or test demonstrates that a multi-bone weighted mesh updates with pose changes.

stories.title: Implement GPU skinning and dynamic mesh rendering
stories.description: Extend the renderer with dynamic vertex buffers and a GPU skinning shader path so weighted meshes can render efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: The renderer uploads dynamic mesh data for weighted attachments.
A GPU skinning shader path exists and is exercised by the sample app.
The sample weighted mesh renders without falling back to a rigid-only path.

stories.title: Implement blend modes and two-color tinting
stories.description: Add slot-level blend mode handling and dual light or dark tint support in the renderer so the runtime matches the documented presentation model.
acceptanceCriteria: Normal, additive, multiply, and screen blend modes are supported for slots.
Two-color tint uniforms or equivalents are wired into the render path.
A sample scene or test demonstrates at least one non-default blend mode and tint combination.

stories.title: Implement FFD deform timelines
stories.description: Add free-form deformation support so mesh vertices can be keyed directly over time for face and soft-body style animation.
acceptanceCriteria: FFD timeline data can be parsed and stored for mesh attachments.
Runtime playback applies deform offsets on top of weighted mesh evaluation.
A sample animation or test demonstrates mesh deformation over time.

stories.title: Implement point and bounding-box attachments
stories.description: Add point attachments for spawn anchors and bounding-box attachments for hit detection data in runtime structures and loading code.
acceptanceCriteria: Point attachments expose world position and rotation from the active pose.
Bounding-box attachment polygons can be parsed and transformed into world space.
Sample data includes at least one point and one bounding-box attachment.

stories.title: Implement SkeletonBounds queries
stories.description: Build the SkeletonBounds utility from the architecture section so hit tests and segment intersections can be queried against transformed bounding boxes.
acceptanceCriteria: SkeletonBounds can update transformed polygons from current pose data.
Point containment and segment intersection queries are supported.
A test or sample command demonstrates at least one positive hit result.

stories.title: Implement clipping and sequence attachments
stories.description: Add clipping polygons for render masking and sequence attachments for frame-based effects so the runtime covers the documented attachment variants.
acceptanceCriteria: Clipping attachment metadata can be loaded and applied in the renderer.
Sequence attachments can advance frames according to fps and playback mode.
A sample scene demonstrates either clipping or sequence playback end to end.

stories.title: Implement IK constraints
stories.description: Add one-bone and two-bone IK solving with bend direction and mix control so limbs can target positions without manual keying.
acceptanceCriteria: IK constraint data can be parsed from fixture content.
Runtime solving supports target, bones, mix, and bend direction behavior.
A sample or test shows an IK chain reaching toward a target.

stories.title: Implement path and transform constraints
stories.description: Add path-following and transform-copy constraints so the runtime can drive chains along curves and mirror transforms between bones.
acceptanceCriteria: Path constraint data can be parsed and evaluated for a sample chain.
Transform constraints can copy rotate, translate, scale, or shear with mix values.
Verification output demonstrates at least one path or transform constraint affecting the pose.

stories.title: Implement physics constraints
stories.description: Add spring-style secondary motion with inertia, damping, strength, gravity, and wind so runtime chains can react dynamically.
acceptanceCriteria: Physics constraint metadata is represented in runtime data structures.
A simulation step updates constrained bones using damping and restoring forces.
A sample or test demonstrates visible or numeric secondary motion.

stories.title: Implement inherit timelines and skin-scoped constraints
stories.description: Add timeline-driven inheritance toggles and skin-specific constraint activation so pose behavior can change at runtime based on animation and active skin.
acceptanceCriteria: Bone inheritance flags can be changed by timeline evaluation.
Constraints and extra bones can be activated conditionally based on the current skin.
A sample or test shows a skin or timeline switching constraint behavior.

stories.title: Implement reverse playback and root motion extraction
stories.description: Add reverse playback flags and root-bone delta extraction so gameplay systems can consume animation-driven movement.
acceptanceCriteria: Animation playback can be reversed without duplicating timeline data.
Runtime code can extract root motion deltas from playback.
A sample or test demonstrates root motion values changing over time.

stories.title: Add runtime inspection and demo CLI
stories.description: Create a terminal-facing inspection command that loads Marrow assets and prints or previews a useful summary for verification in future loop runs.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented command can inspect an .mskl file from the terminal.
The command prints skeleton name, counts, available animations, and active skins.
The command or sample app is usable as a smoke test in later stories.

stories.title: Implement binary export and import groundwork
stories.description: Add a binary serialization path for production assets using the documented compact encoding strategy while keeping JSON fixtures for debugging.
acceptanceCriteria: A binary asset format and file extension are documented in code or docs.
The runtime can load a binary form of at least one sample skeleton.
JSON and binary sample outputs can be compared through a validation command or test.

stories.title: Implement .marrow project model
stories.description: Define and load the editor-side project file model that references skeleton data, atlas assets, and authoring metadata for the standalone Marrow editor.
acceptanceCriteria: A .marrow project structure is represented in code.
The editor can create or load a minimal project that points at sample assets.
The project model clearly separates editor metadata from exported runtime data.

stories.title: Bootstrap the Dear ImGui editor shell
stories.description: Create the standalone editor application using C++, Dear ImGui, GLFW, and OpenGL so authoring tools have a running host UI.
acceptanceCriteria: A desktop editor executable builds and opens a window.
The editor integrates Dear ImGui, GLFW, and OpenGL according to the documented stack.
AGENTS.md documents how to launch the editor.

stories.title: Build the editor viewport and skeleton inspector
stories.description: Add a viewport and inspector panels so users can load a project, inspect bones, and view the current setup pose inside the editor shell.
acceptanceCriteria: The editor can open the sample project and display the skeleton in a viewport.
An inspector can list bones and show key setup data for the selected item.
Selection in the viewport or hierarchy updates the inspector state.

stories.title: Build slot, attachment, and skin inspectors
stories.description: Add editor panels for slots, attachments, skins, and linked meshes so presentation data can be browsed and edited in the standalone app.
acceptanceCriteria: The editor can inspect slots and attachments from the sample project.
Skin selection and preview are available in the editor.
Linked mesh relationships are visible or editable in the UI.

stories.title: Build the animation timeline shell
stories.description: Implement the core timeline UI needed to scrub time, select animations, and manage keyed tracks inside the editor.
acceptanceCriteria: The editor can select an animation and scrub playback time.
A timeline panel shows keyed tracks for at least one animation.
Timeline state stays in sync with the viewport pose.

stories.title: Implement keyframe and curve editing
stories.description: Add authoring tools for transform keys with linear, stepped, and bezier interpolation editing so the editor matches the documented keyframe model.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can add or edit transform keys in the timeline.
Interpolation mode can be switched between linear, stepped, and bezier.
Edited curves round-trip through exported asset data.

stories.title: Implement mesh weighting and FFD authoring tools
stories.description: Add editor workflows for weighted mesh assignment and free-form deformation key authoring so advanced mesh animation can be created in-app.
acceptanceCriteria: The editor provides a workflow for assigning or visualizing mesh weights.
FFD key data can be authored or edited in the editor.
Runtime playback can verify the exported weighted mesh and FFD data.

stories.title: Implement constraint authoring panels
stories.description: Add editor workflows for IK, path, transform, and physics constraints so the documented runtime features can be authored visually.
acceptanceCriteria: The editor can create or edit IK constraints.
The editor can create or edit path, transform, and physics constraints.
Edited constraints round-trip into exported runtime data and preview correctly.

stories.title: Implement event, draw-order, and state-preview authoring
stories.description: Add editor tools for event keys, draw-order editing, track mixing preview, and root-motion-aware playback so users can author the higher-level animation state behaviors.
acceptanceCriteria: The editor can place event keys and draw-order changes on the timeline.
Track mixing or queued playback can be previewed in the editor.
Root motion or reverse playback behavior can be previewed from the editor.

stories.title: Implement export pipeline from editor to runtime assets
stories.description: Wire the editor to export .mskl, .matl, texture atlas assets, and optional binary output so authored content can be consumed directly by the runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: The editor can export runtime assets from a .marrow project.
Exported assets load successfully through the runtime inspection or sample app path.
The export flow supports JSON output and optional binary output.

stories.title: Ship an end-to-end Marrow sample project
stories.description: Create a representative sample project and validation checklist that proves the runtime and editor can author, export, load, and render a non-trivial animated asset end to end.
acceptanceCriteria: A sample .marrow project is checked into the repository.
The sample can be exported and loaded by the runtime without manual patching.
Repository docs explain the end-to-end validation flow for new contributors.

stories.title: Fix bone inherit modes for non-uniform parent scale
stories.description: Research confirmed Marrow's shear formula matches Spine exactly. However, Spine has 5 discrete inherit modes (NORMAL, ONLY_TRANSLATION, NO_ROTATION_OR_REFLECTION, NO_SCALE, NO_SCALE_OR_REFLECTION) while Marrow maps them to 3 booleans, producing incorrect transforms when parent has non-uniform scale. Implement all 5 modes matching Spine's Bone.c updateWorldTransformWith(). Also add skeleton-level scaleX/scaleY that Spine applies to root bones.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 Spine inherit modes are implemented as a discrete enum, not boolean flags.
NO_ROTATION_OR_REFLECTION uses Spine's cross-term correction: s = abs(pa*pd - pb*pc) / (pa*pa + pc*pc).
NO_SCALE and NO_SCALE_OR_REFLECTION use Spine's axis rotation + length normalization algorithm.
Skeleton-level scaleX/scaleY is applied to root bone transforms.
A test with non-uniform parent scale validates each inherit mode produces correct transforms.
Existing fixture smoke tests pass unchanged.

stories.title: Rewrite IK constraint solver to match Spine reference
stories.description: Research found 7 discrepancies vs Spine's IK solver. Rewrite both one-bone and two-bone IK: (1) Add softness parameter for smooth near-full-extension. (2) Add stretch/compress support. (3) Implement non-uniform scale ellipse solver with quartic fallback (Spine uses a completely different path when parent scaleX != scaleY). (4) Handle negative bone scale (add 180° flip). (5) Zero-length parent falls back to single-bone IK instead of skipping. (6) Inherit-mode-aware target transforms for one-bone IK. (7) Guard division by zero with epsilon fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-bone IK does not produce NaN when target overlaps the chain root.
Softness parameter smooths the snap at full extension (test with softness=20).
Non-uniform parent scale (scaleX=1, scaleY=2) produces correct IK solution via ellipse solver.
Negative bone scaleX flips IK rotation by 180°.
Zero-length parent bone falls back to single-bone IK, not skip.
All angle results are wrapped to [-180, 180].
A comprehensive IK test suite covers: reachable, unreachable, degenerate, non-uniform, negative scale, softness cases.

stories.title: Implement Spine-compatible rotation mixing with cross-detection
stories.description: Research found Spine uses a sophisticated cross-detection algorithm for rotation mixing (not simple angle wrapping). Spine maintains a per-timeline timelinesRotation[] array that tracks accumulated rotation and detects direction crosses at the ±180° boundary. Simple wrapping causes visible rotation pops during crossfades. Implement Spine's _spAnimationState_applyRotateTimeline algorithm. Also ensure rotation keyframes store deltas relative to setup pose, matching Spine's getRelativeValue pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: AnimationState maintains a timelinesRotation array per track for cross-detection.
Rotation from 350° to 10° interpolates the short way (20° arc) during mixing.
Crossing the ±180° boundary mid-crossfade does not cause a rotation pop.
The O(1) normalization formula (ceil(diff/360 - 0.5) * 360) replaces the while loop.
A test with a 3-keyframe rotation (0° → 170° → -170°) produces smooth continuous rotation.
Existing rotation smoke tests pass.

stories.title: Normalize mesh vertex weights on load
stories.description: Mesh weight parsing validates that weights sum to positive but does not normalize to 1.0. GPU skinning (renderer/module.cpp) assumes normalized weights. Add normalization at parse time and assert the invariant in the renderer.
acceptanceCriteria: Mesh vertex weights are normalized to sum=1.0 during skeleton loading.
The renderer asserts or documents that incoming weights are pre-normalized.
A fixture mesh with intentionally non-unit weights (e.g., 0.3+0.5=0.8) loads correctly after normalization.
Existing weighted mesh smoke tests still pass.

stories.title: Rewrite physics constraints to Spine's offset-based model
stories.description: Research found Marrow's physics model is architecturally different from Spine. Spine uses offset-based simulation (tracks deviation from animated pose) with fixed timestep accumulator and 4 independent channels (X/Y position, rotation, scale). Marrow uses position-tracking spring with variable timestep, producing frame-rate-dependent results. Rewrite to match Spine's PhysicsConstraint.c: (1) Offset-based model with xOffset/yOffset/rotateOffset/scaleOffset. (2) Fixed timestep accumulator (step parameter). (3) Previous-position tracking (ux/uy, cx/cy). (4) Inertia limiting (limit * delta). (5) SP_PHYSICS_POSE mode for applying offsets without simulating. (6) Proper reset/initialization.
acceptanceCriteria: Physics simulation uses fixed timestep accumulator (configurable step size).
Position, rotation, and scale are simulated as independent offset channels.
Frame rate does not affect physics behavior (test at 30fps vs 120fps produces same result at t=1.0).
Inertia is clamped by limit * delta to prevent instability during frame spikes.
reset() snapshots current world position and zeroes all velocities/offsets.
POSE mode applies accumulated offsets without advancing simulation.
Existing physics fixture assertions pass.

stories.title: Fix transform constraint bounds check and event timeline ordering
stories.description: Transform constraint solver (skeleton.cpp:6216) accesses data_->bones()[bone_index] without prior bounds validation. Event timeline (skeleton.cpp:2447) uses < instead of <= for timestamp ordering, inconsistent with other timelines. Fix both issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Transform constraint validates bone_index before accessing bones().
Event timeline uses <= for timestamp monotonicity, consistent with all other timeline types.
Invalid bone index in a transform constraint produces a clear error instead of UB.
A test with two events at the same timestamp fires both in correct order.

stories.title: Implement linked mesh FFD deform inheritance
stories.description: Linked meshes with deform=true should inherit FFD vertex offsets from their parent mesh during animation. Currently the deform flag is parsed but parent FFD offsets are not propagated. Wire deform inheritance through the mesh deformation pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: A linked mesh with deform=true applies the parent mesh's FFD offsets during animation.
A linked mesh with deform=false stays rigid regardless of parent deformation.
A new fixture linked mesh pair validates both cases.
Existing mesh deform smoke tests still pass.

stories.title: Implement OpenGL shader pipeline for region attachments
stories.description: The renderer currently only prepares draw command data but has no actual GL calls. Create the minimal OpenGL rendering pipeline: compile a textured-quad vertex/fragment shader pair, set up VAO/VBO, and render region attachments as textured quads using the prepared draw commands.
acceptanceCriteria: A vertex shader transforms 2D positions and passes UVs to the fragment shader.
A fragment shader samples a texture and applies slot color tint.
marrow_renderer_sample opens a GLFW window and renders the fixture skeleton's region attachments as colored quads (no texture file needed — use a solid white 1x1 texture fallback).
The window stays open until closed by the user.

stories.title: Implement OpenGL blend modes and two-color tinting shader
stories.description: Extend the GL pipeline with per-draw-command blend mode switching (glBlendFunc for Normal/Additive/Multiply/Screen) and a two-color tint shader variant: output = texColor * light + (1 - texColor.a) * dark.
acceptanceCriteria: Each draw command applies the correct glBlendFunc before rendering.
A two-color tint shader path is available and activated when dark_color is non-default.
marrow_renderer_sample visually demonstrates at least one non-Normal blend mode.
A smoke assertion validates that additive and screen blend produce different framebuffer results than normal.

stories.title: Implement OpenGL dynamic mesh rendering with GPU skinning
stories.description: Extend the GL pipeline to upload DynamicMeshDrawCommand vertex/index data into VBOs and render skinned meshes. Use the pre-computed CPU-side skinned vertices from the renderer module.
acceptanceCriteria: Dynamic mesh vertex data is uploaded to a GL VBO each frame.
Index data drives GL draw calls for skinned mesh triangles.
marrow_renderer_sample renders the fixture weighted mesh alongside region attachments.
Mesh vertices update visually when the animation pose changes.

stories.title: Implement texture atlas loading into GL textures
stories.description: Load the atlas image file referenced in .matl into a GL texture so region and mesh attachments render with actual texture data instead of a fallback. Support PNG loading via stb_image or a minimal image loader.
acceptanceCriteria: The atlas image path from .matl is loaded into a GL texture at startup.
Region attachment UVs correctly sample from the loaded atlas texture.
A sample atlas PNG is checked into assets/ for validation.
Fallback to white texture when the image file is missing.

stories.title: Implement sequence attachment frame advancement in renderer
stories.description: Sequence attachments (sprite sheet animations like spark_fx) are parsed and stored but the renderer does not advance frames based on fps and playback mode. Wire sequence frame selection into the render preparation path.
acceptanceCriteria: Sequence attachments select the correct frame region based on animation time and configured fps.
Playback modes (loop, once, pingpong) are respected.
marrow_fixture_smoke validates sequence frame selection at multiple time points.
The renderer uses the correct atlas region for the active sequence frame.

stories.title: Add undo/redo to the editor
stories.description: Implement an undo/redo command stack in the editor so all project modifications (keyframe edits, constraint changes, property edits) can be reverted. Use a command-pattern approach with serializable operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Every editor modification pushes an undoable command onto the stack.
Ctrl+Z undoes the last command and restores the previous state.
Ctrl+Shift+Z or Ctrl+Y redoes a previously undone command.
The editor project smoke test validates at least one undo/redo cycle.

stories.title: Replace per-frame cycle detection with topological bone sort
stories.description: update_world_transforms() currently runs cycle detection with evaluated/evaluating vectors every frame. For large skeletons at 60fps this adds overhead. Compute a topological bone evaluation order once at load time and reuse it for all subsequent updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Bone evaluation order is computed once during SkeletonData loading.
update_world_transforms() iterates the pre-sorted order without per-frame cycle detection.
Circular bone hierarchies are detected and rejected at load time with a clear error.
All existing fixture smoke tests produce identical world transforms.

stories.title: Split skeleton.cpp into focused modules
stories.description: skeleton.cpp is 7000+ lines covering parsing, constraints, skins, animation application, and physics. Split into: skeleton_parse.cpp, skeleton_constraints.cpp, skeleton_skin.cpp, skeleton_animation.cpp, and skeleton_physics.cpp while keeping the same public API.
acceptanceCriteria: skeleton.cpp is split into 5+ focused source files.
include/marrow/runtime/skeleton.hpp public API is unchanged.
All existing tests compile and pass without modification.
CMakeLists.txt is updated with the new source files.

stories.title: Add error reporting for silent failure paths
stories.description: Several code paths silently fail: GPU skinning returns empty vector on bad bone index (renderer/module.cpp:267), empty attachment name silently replaces (skeleton.cpp:3232). Convert these to explicit error reporting via std::optional<std::string> or error callbacks.
acceptanceCriteria: GPU skinning evaluation reports an error on invalid bone index instead of returning empty.
Attachment operations report errors on invalid names instead of silently proceeding.
At least 3 previously-silent error paths now produce actionable messages.
Error paths are exercised by new negative test cases in the smoke test.

stories.title: Add comprehensive unit tests for runtime math
stories.description: The current test coverage is limited to 2 smoke test files. Add focused unit tests for: interpolation edge cases, matrix composition, IK solving, physics stepping, and SkeletonBounds queries. Use a lightweight test framework or plain assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test target (marrow_unit_tests) exists in CMakeLists.txt.
Interpolation tests cover: boundary values (alpha=0, 1), bezier extremes, stepped transitions.
Matrix tests cover: identity, pure rotation, pure scale, shear, parent-child composition.
IK tests cover: reachable, unreachable, degenerate, and wrap-around cases.
All tests pass and are documented in AGENTS.md.

stories.title: Add .mskl/.matl format version header and migration check
stories.description: Current format files have no version header, making future format changes risky. Add a 'version' field to .mskl and .matl root objects, validate on load, and reject unsupported versions with a clear message.
acceptanceCriteria: All .mskl and .matl fixture files include a 'version' field.
The loader validates the version and rejects unknown versions with an actionable error.
A test with a future version number (e.g., 99) produces a clear rejection message.
Existing fixtures load successfully after adding the version field.

stories.title: Implement AnimationState timeline mode system
stories.description: Research found Spine uses SUBSEQUENT/FIRST/HOLD_SUBSEQUENT/HOLD_FIRST/HOLD_MIX modes per timeline to control how each property blends during mixing. This determines whether a timeline uses setup pose or current pose as the base. Marrow has none of this, using flat weight-based blend. Also add: trackEnd for empty animation auto-clear, interruptAlpha for smooth crossfade interruption, mixingTo back-pointer for doubly-linked mixing chain, never-applied entry detection (nextTrackLast == -1).
acceptanceCriteria: Each timeline in a TrackEntry has an assigned mode (SUBSEQUENT/FIRST/HOLD_SUBSEQUENT/HOLD_FIRST/HOLD_MIX).
Timeline modes are computed via _computeHold using the mixingTo chain.
TrackEntry has trackEnd field; empty animations auto-clear when trackLast >= trackEnd.
interruptAlpha preserves mix progress when a crossfade is interrupted.
Entries that were never applied (nextTrackLast == -1) skip mixing-from.
A test interrupting a crossfade mid-transition produces smooth result, not a pop.
A test validates empty animation fades to setup pose and auto-clears the track.

stories.title: Implement streaming VBO batch renderer with draw call merging
stories.description: Research shows production Spine renderers use a single streaming VBO batcher that merges consecutive draw commands sharing the same (texture, blend_mode) pair. Marrow's PreparedScene currently separates region and mesh attachments. Unify into a single slot-ordered draw list, allocate one large VBO (~2MB), map + append vertices each frame, and issue glDrawElements per batch boundary. Use mat3x2 for bone palette uniforms (half the cost of mat4).
acceptanceCriteria: PreparedScene produces a single interleaved draw command list ordered by slot index.
Consecutive commands with same texture and blend mode are merged into one batch.
A streaming VBO (GL_STREAM_DRAW) uploads all vertices in one glBufferSubData per frame.
Bone palette uses mat3x2 uniforms (6 floats per bone, not 16).
A renderer sample with multiple skeletons demonstrates cross-skeleton batching.
Draw call count is logged and verified to be minimal.

stories.title: Implement two-color tint shader with PMA support
stories.description: Research found the exact Spine two-color tint formula: output.rgb = (texColor.a - texColor.rgb) * dark.rgb + texColor.rgb * light.rgb for PMA textures. Also support a u_pma toggle for straight-alpha: output.rgb = (u_pma + (1-u_pma)*texColor.a - texColor.rgb) * dark + texColor.rgb * light. Dark color must be a per-vertex attribute (not uniform) for correct batching across slots with different dark colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Fragment shader implements the exact Spine PMA two-color tint formula.
dark_color is passed as a per-vertex attribute (4 x uint8 normalized).
A u_pma uniform toggles between premultiplied and straight alpha modes.
PMA blend mode uses glBlendFunc(GL_ONE, GL_ONE_MINUS_SRC_ALPHA).
Renderer sample demonstrates visible two-color tinting on at least one slot.
Non-tinted slots use a simplified shader path (skip dark color reads).

stories.title: Implement GPU stencil-based clipping for clip attachments
stories.description: Research shows GPU stencil clipping is simpler and more performant than CPU polygon clipping for Spine-like runtimes. Algorithm: (1) flush batch, (2) disable color writes, (3) clear stencil, (4) set stencil op to INVERT, (5) draw clip polygon as triangle fan, (6) enable color + stencil test NOTEQUAL, (7) draw clipped attachments, (8) disable stencil. This handles concave polygons natively via stencil inversion.
acceptanceCriteria: GLFW window requests 8-bit stencil buffer (GLFW_STENCIL_BITS, 8).
Clipping polygon renders to stencil buffer using GL_INVERT with triangle fan.
Draw commands between clip-start and clip-end are stencil-tested.
Stencil state is restored after clip range ends.
A test with a concave clipping polygon correctly masks interior attachments.
Nested clipping uses incremented stencil reference values.

stories.title: Implement editor viewport with FBO, docking, and bone picking
stories.description: Research provides exact patterns for ImGui FBO viewport, DockBuilder layout, and bone picking. Wire GL rendering into an offscreen FBO, display via ImGui::Image with UV flip, use DockSpaceOverViewport + DockBuilder for default panel layout (Viewport/Timeline/Hierarchy/Properties), and implement point-to-segment bone picking in screen space with joint priority.
acceptanceCriteria: Editor renders skeleton into an FBO and displays it as ImGui::Image in the viewport panel.
UV coordinates are Y-flipped for OpenGL convention (ImVec2(0,1) to ImVec2(1,0)).
FBO resizes when the viewport panel resizes.
DockBuilder creates default layout: Viewport center, Timeline bottom, Hierarchy left-top, Properties left-bottom.
Clicking in viewport picks the nearest bone using screen-space point-to-segment distance.
Joint hit zones (6px radius) take priority over bone body hit zones (8px threshold).
Selected bone highlights in viewport and syncs with inspector.

stories.title: Implement editor undo/redo with command pattern
stories.description: Research provides the ImGui-specific undo/redo pattern: use EditAction command objects with undo()/redo() methods, IsItemDeactivatedAfterEdit() to coalesce continuous drags into single undo entries, and merge-by-group for related edits. Implement for: bone transform edits, keyframe add/remove/modify, constraint property changes, and skin/attachment swaps.
acceptanceCriteria: UndoStack maintains undo and redo vectors of EditAction objects (max depth 100).
Ctrl+Z undoes, Ctrl+Shift+Z redoes.
ImGui sliders/drags use IsItemDeactivatedAfterEdit to coalesce into one undo entry.
At least 4 action types implemented: MoveBone, AddKeyframe, RemoveKeyframe, EditProperty.
Redo stack clears when a new action is executed after undo.
Editor smoke test validates a full undo/redo cycle.

stories.title: Add binary format keyframe quantization and key reduction
stories.description: Research from Riot Games shows 48-bit quantized keyframes (16-bit time + 16-bit channel index + 32-bit payload for 2D) with iterative Bezier curve fitting can reduce animation data by 50-70%. Implement at export time: (1) Quantize rotation to 16-bit, position to 2x16-bit. (2) Curve fit to remove redundant keyframes within error threshold. (3) Store all keyframes contiguously sorted by time for cache-friendly playback.
acceptanceCriteria: Export pipeline quantizes rotation keyframes to 16-bit (±0.006° precision).
Position keyframes quantize to 2x16-bit with animation bounding box range reduction.
Key reduction removes redundant keyframes within configurable error threshold.
Binary format stores keyframes contiguously sorted by (time, bone_index).
Roundtrip test: original animation vs quantized animation has max error < 0.1° rotation, < 0.5px position.
Size comparison logged: binary-quantized vs JSON.

stories.title: Extract RenderCommand abstraction from monolithic GL renderer
stories.description: Refactor module.cpp to separate draw command generation (what to draw) from GL submission (how to draw), following Spine's RenderCommand pattern. The renderer should produce a CommandList of (vertices, indices, texture handle, blend mode) tuples that any backend can consume. This is the prerequisite for multi-backend support.
acceptanceCriteria: A RenderCommandList struct holds ordered draw commands with vertex data, index data, texture handle, and blend mode.
The existing GL code consumes RenderCommandList through a Backend interface.
No GL calls exist outside the OpenGL backend implementation.
All existing renderer_sample and smoke tests pass unchanged.
The Backend interface has create/destroy, begin_frame/end_frame, submit_commands methods.

stories.title: Integrate sokol_gfx as multi-backend renderer
stories.description: Research recommends sokol_gfx for the best coverage-to-complexity ratio: Metal, Vulkan, GL, GLES, WebGL, D3D11 from single-header C. Replace the direct GL backend with sokol_gfx calls. Use sokol-shdc to cross-compile the existing GLSL into a C header with per-backend shader bytecode (GLSL, MSL, HLSL, SPIR-V, WGSL).
acceptanceCriteria: sokol_gfx.h and sokol_app.h are vendored in external/.
sokol-shdc compiles the vertex/fragment shaders into a multi-backend C header.
The SokolBackend implementation consumes RenderCommandList from MAR-067.
marrow_renderer_sample renders correctly using sokol_gfx on the current platform.
Build system detects the platform and selects the appropriate backend (Metal on macOS, GL on Linux).

stories.title: Verify Metal rendering on macOS via sokol_gfx
stories.description: macOS deprecated OpenGL. Verify the sokol_gfx Metal backend renders correctly: textured quads, blend modes, two-color tint, stencil clipping, GPU skinned meshes. Fix any platform-specific issues.
acceptanceCriteria: marrow_renderer_sample renders on macOS using the Metal backend (no GL fallback).
All 4 blend modes (Normal/Additive/Multiply/Screen) produce correct output.
Two-color tint and stencil clipping work on Metal.
The editor shell renders the viewport via Metal.
No OpenGL deprecation warnings in the build.

stories.title: Create C API wrapper for FFI (marrow_c.h)
stories.description: Expose the C++ runtime as a stable C API for game engine bindings (Unity C#, Godot GDExtension, Rust FFI). Use opaque handle types (MarrowSkeleton*, MarrowAnimState*), extern C free functions, MarrowStatusCode error returns, and callback-based event listeners with function_pointer + void* user_data.
acceptanceCriteria: include/marrow/marrow_c.h provides the complete public API as extern C functions.
Each C++ class has a distinct opaque handle type (not void*).
All functions return MarrowStatusCode; no exceptions cross the C boundary.
Event listeners use function_pointer + void* user_data pattern.
A C-only smoke test (no C++ includes) compiles and exercises load/animate/render.
ABI version check function prevents mismatched header/library usage.

stories.title: Add custom memory allocator support
stories.description: Game engines require custom allocators for console/mobile. Add a marrow::Allocator interface with allocate(size, alignment)/deallocate(ptr, size), settable globally at startup. Route all internal new/delete through it. Expose as MarrowAllocator callback struct in the C API.
acceptanceCriteria: marrow::Allocator virtual interface with allocate/deallocate methods.
Global default allocator uses std::malloc/free.
set_allocator() replaces the global allocator before any Marrow calls.
All runtime heap allocations go through the allocator (no direct new/delete).
A test with a counting allocator verifies zero leaked allocations after full lifecycle.
C API exposes MarrowAllocator struct with function pointers.

stories.title: Ensure thread-safe concurrent skeleton updates
stories.description: Game engines parallelize skeleton updates across threads. Audit the runtime for global mutable state. Document the contract: distinct Skeleton+AnimationState instances can be updated concurrently from different threads. SkeletonData is immutable and safely shared.
acceptanceCriteria: No static mutable state exists in the animation/skeleton update hot path.
SkeletonData is documented as thread-safe for concurrent read access.
A stress test spawns N threads, each updating a unique Skeleton instance from shared SkeletonData.
No data races detected under ThreadSanitizer.
The threading contract is documented in the public API headers.

stories.title: Implement hot-reload for skeleton assets
stories.description: Allow re-loading .mskl/.matl at runtime without restarting. Implement capture_state()/restore_state() on AnimationState to preserve playback position across reloads. The editor should auto-reload when source files change.
acceptanceCriteria: AnimationState::capture_state() returns a serializable snapshot of track configuration.
AnimationState::restore_state() resumes playback from a snapshot after SkeletonData rebuild.
The editor watches .mskl/.matl files and triggers hot-reload on change.
Hot-reload preserves current animation, track time, and mix state.
A smoke test validates: load → play → modify .mskl → reload → verify playback continues.

stories.title: Implement Spine JSON importer
stories.description: Parse Spine's JSON export format (.json) and convert to Marrow's .mskl format. Legal: file formats are not copyrightable; our own parser is fine. Target Spine 4.x JSON. Implement in phases: bones/slots/skins first, then animations, then constraints. Handle the tricky weighted mesh vertex encoding where format switches based on len(vertices) > len(uvs).
acceptanceCriteria: A spine_to_marrow CLI tool converts Spine .json to .mskl.
Bones, slots, skins, and basic region/mesh attachments convert correctly.
Animation timelines (rotate, translate, scale, color, attachment) convert correctly.
Weighted mesh vertex encoding is handled for both weighted and unweighted cases.
IK, path, and transform constraints convert to Marrow equivalents.
A test converts a known Spine sample skeleton and validates bone positions match.

stories.title: Implement Spine atlas importer
stories.description: Parse Spine's line-based .atlas text format and convert to Marrow's .matl JSON. Handle atlas page headers, region definitions, rotation flags (Spine uses 90° CW), filter/wrap modes, and multi-page atlases.
acceptanceCriteria: The spine_to_marrow tool also converts .atlas to .matl.
Atlas page metadata (image name, size, filter, wrap) converts correctly.
Region definitions (name, bounds, origin, rotation) convert correctly.
Spine's 90° CW rotation flag is handled (UVs adjusted or flag preserved).
Multi-page atlases produce multiple .matl files or a combined document.
A test converts a known Spine atlas and validates region lookups match.

stories.title: Implement texture atlas packer in the editor
stories.description: Add an atlas packing tool using the MaxRects Best Short Side Fit algorithm. Support padding, trim/whitespace stripping, and bleed/extrude. Use stb_rect_pack for the initial implementation, upgradeable to MaxRectsBinPack for rotation support.
acceptanceCriteria: The editor can generate a .matl + packed PNG from individual sprite images.
MaxRects BSSF algorithm produces compact packing.
Padding (configurable, default 2px) prevents texture bleeding.
Trim removes transparent borders and records offset for correct rendering.
Bleed/extrude extends edge pixels by 1px to prevent seam artifacts.
A test packs 20+ regions and validates the output atlas loads correctly.

stories.title: Implement SoA bone data layout with SIMD transform propagation
stories.description: Convert bone arrays from AoS to SoA layout: separate float arrays for worldX, worldY, a, b, c, d. Implement SSE2 and NEON code paths for batch bone transform propagation (4 bones simultaneously). Store bones in topological order so parent_index < child_index always holds.
acceptanceCriteria: Bone world transforms are stored as 6 separate float arrays (SoA layout).
SSE2 path processes 4 bones per iteration on x86-64.
NEON path processes 4 bones per iteration on ARM/Apple Silicon.
Scalar fallback exists for unsupported platforms.
marrow_benchmark shows measurable speedup (>2x) for 100+ bone skeletons.
All existing smoke tests produce identical results.

stories.title: Add frustum culling and update-rate throttling
stories.description: Add visibility culling and animation update rate control as first-class runtime features. Invisible skeletons skip all update work. Distant skeletons update at reduced frequency with pose interpolation.
acceptanceCriteria: set_visible(false) skips animation apply, world transform, and render for a skeleton.
set_update_interval(n) updates animation every nth frame, interpolating between known poses.
Culling reduces per-skeleton cost to near-zero (verified by profiler).
Throttled update at interval=3 produces visually acceptable results.
A stress test with 200 skeletons (100 culled, 50 throttled) runs at 60fps.

stories.title: Build marrow_benchmark stress test harness
stories.description: Create a benchmark binary that spawns N skeletons with configurable bone count and measures frame time. Report per-skeleton breakdown: animation apply, world transform, mesh skinning, constraint solving, render prepare. Produce a 0-100 performance score.
acceptanceCriteria: marrow_benchmark spawns N skeletons (default 100) with configurable complexity.
Per-frame timing breakdown printed: animation_us, transform_us, skinning_us, constraint_us, render_us.
Total frame time and estimated max skeletons at 60fps reported.
Performance score 0-100 based on target: 200 medium skeletons at 60fps = 100.
Results are reproducible (variance < 10% across runs).
AGENTS.md documents the benchmark command.

stories.title: Implement onion skinning in the editor viewport
stories.description: Show ghost frames before/after the current timeline position. Support frame-based and keyframe-based modes, anchor mode for stable arcs, and separate before/after colors (cool/warm). Render as semi-transparent tinted pass. Limit to 3+3 ghosts default.
acceptanceCriteria: Frame-based ghosts show N frames before and after current time with configurable step.
Keyframe-based ghosts show only at actual keyframe positions.
Anchor mode shows ghosts at fixed intervals relative to frame 0.
Before ghosts render in blue tint, after ghosts in red/warm tint.
Alpha decreases with distance from current frame.
Default is 3 before + 3 after; user-configurable up to 6+6.
Toggle on/off via editor menu.

stories.title: Implement brush-based weight painting in the editor
stories.description: Add a weight painting tool for mesh bone influences. Brush modes: paint (add weight), erase (remove weight), smooth (average neighbors). Display weights as heat map overlay (blue=0, red=1). This is the most requested feature across all 2D animation communities.
acceptanceCriteria: Paint mode adds bone influence weight under brush with configurable radius and strength.
Erase mode removes weight from the active bone.
Smooth mode averages weights with neighboring vertices.
Heat map overlay shows current weights (blue→green→yellow→red gradient).
Weights auto-normalize to sum=1.0 after each stroke.
Undo/redo works for each paint stroke.
Real-time mesh deformation preview while painting.

stories.title: Implement PSD layer import pipeline
stories.description: Import Photoshop PSD files as individual sprite layers, automatically creating slots and region attachments from layer names. This is the standard 2D animation art pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: PSD file can be loaded and individual layers extracted as images.
Layer names map to slot/attachment names in the skeleton.
Layer positions preserved as attachment offsets.
Groups/folders map to bone hierarchy hints.
Re-import updates existing attachments without losing animation data.
A test imports a sample PSD and validates slot creation.

stories.title: Add animation layers (additive and override)
stories.description: Support non-destructive animation layering: base layer plays the main animation, additive layers modify on top (e.g., breathing overlay), override layers replace specific bones (e.g., aim direction). Per-layer weight control.
acceptanceCriteria: AnimationState supports multiple layers with configurable blend mode (additive/override).
Each layer has an alpha weight (0.0 to 1.0).
Additive layers add their deltas on top of lower layers.
Override layers replace bone transforms for specified bones.
A test plays walk + breathing additive + aim override simultaneously.
Performance impact of 3 active layers is < 50% overhead vs single layer.

stories.title: Implement debug overlay for bones, constraints, and meshes
stories.description: Add a debug rendering mode that draws bone hierarchy lines, IK reach cones, path constraint splines, physics spring visualization, mesh wireframes, and bounding boxes. Toggle per category in the editor.
acceptanceCriteria: Bone hierarchy renders as colored lines (parent→child) with joint circles.
IK constraints show target marker and reach limit arc.
Path constraints show the bezier spline curve.
Physics constraints show spring/damper visualization.
Mesh wireframe overlay can be toggled.
Bounding box polygons render as semi-transparent outlines.
Each debug category can be toggled independently in the editor.

stories.title: Add runtime profiling instrumentation and HUD overlay
stories.description: Instrument the runtime with per-frame timing and provide a text HUD overlay. Report: skeleton count, draw calls, vertex count, and per-phase timing (animation, transform, skinning, constraints, render).
acceptanceCriteria: marrow_profiler_frame() returns a struct with per-phase microsecond timings.
A text HUD overlay can be toggled in the editor and renderer_sample.
HUD shows: skeletons, draw calls, vertices, batch merges, total frame time.
Draw call batching statistics include break reasons (texture/blend/clip changes).
Profiling overhead is < 1% when enabled, zero when disabled.
marrow_benchmark uses the profiler to generate its report.

stories.title: Write Quick Start guide and API concepts documentation
stories.description: Create the most important documentation pages: a Quick Start (load → animate → render in one page) and a Concepts page explaining SkeletonData/Skeleton/AnimationState architecture. Add Doxygen comments to all public API headers.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/quick-start.md shows a complete working example from zero to rendered animation.
docs/concepts.md explains the SkeletonData→Skeleton→AnimationState→Renderer data flow.
All public functions in include/marrow/ have @brief and @param Doxygen comments.
Doxygen builds with zero warnings.
docs/format-spec.md documents .mskl, .matl, .mbin, and .marrow file formats.
AGENTS.md links to all documentation entry points.

stories.title: Fix constraint solver: subtree-only world transform recompute
stories.description: Performance analysis found compute_world_transforms() is called inside every constraint iteration, recomputing ALL 64 bones when only 1-2 changed. skeleton_constraints.cpp lines 225, 317, 731, 749 each trigger a full skeleton rebuild. This is the single largest CPU cost (120us of 151us constraint budget). Apply the recompute_world_subtree pattern (already used for physics at line 775) to IK, path, and transform constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: IK constraints only recompute world transforms for the affected 1-2 bones and their descendants.
Path constraints only recompute the bone chain members.
Transform constraints only recompute the target bone subtree.
compute_world_transforms (full skeleton) is called at most ONCE per update, not per constraint.
marrow_benchmark constraint_us drops from ~151us to <30us for 64-bone 11-constraint skeleton.
All fixture smoke tests produce identical world transforms.

stories.title: Fix SIMD: eliminate double-work AoS recompute after SIMD path
stories.description: Performance analysis found the SIMD path in skeleton_world.cpp lines 357-368 performs SIMD computation into SoA buffers, then ALSO recomputes via scalar compose_world_transform for the AoS buffer. Since AoS pointer is always non-null during constraint solving, every SIMD batch does double work, making NEON 0.75x SLOWER than scalar. Fix: after SIMD stores, copy SoA float results to AoS double buffer directly instead of recomputing.
acceptanceCriteria: propagate_simd_normal_batch copies SoA results to AoS buffer, does not call compose_world_transform.
Same fix applied to propagate_simd_translation_batch.
SIMD NEON benchmark shows >1.5x speedup over scalar (was 0.75x).
All fixture smoke tests produce identical results within float precision tolerance.
marrow_benchmark transform_us shows measurable reduction.

stories.title: Precompute bone children map and tip cache
stories.description: Performance analysis found bone_tip_local_vector (skeleton_constraints.cpp:90-110) does O(B) linear scan to find children, called per IK/physics constraint. Also recompute_world_subtree (line 775) scans all bones per recursion level. Precompute a children adjacency list and bone tip cache at skeleton construction time.
acceptanceCriteria: SkeletonData stores a children_map: parent_index → vector<child_index>.
bone_tip_local_vector uses the children_map for O(1) lookup.
recompute_world_subtree iterates children_map instead of scanning all bones.
Physics subtree recompute is O(subtree_size) instead of O(B * depth).
marrow_benchmark shows reduced constraint_us and transform_us.

stories.title: Cache PreparedScene and dirty-flag render updates
stories.description: Performance analysis found render preparation rebuilds the full PreparedScene every frame (224us), including all draw commands, vertex payloads, and polygon clipping. Most data is invariant when attachments don't change. Cache the scene and only update the bone palette and changed slots via dirty flags.
acceptanceCriteria: PreparedScene is cached between frames.
When no attachment/draw-order changes occurred, only bone_palette is updated (memcpy).
Dirty flags track slot attachment changes, draw order changes, and skin swaps.
render_us drops from ~224us to <40us for frames without attachment changes.
marrow_benchmark score increases significantly.
Attachment swap still triggers a full rebuild for affected slots.

stories.title: Eliminate per-frame allocations in constraint hot path
stories.description: Performance analysis found: solved_poses copies full vector every frame (4KB for 64 bones), path constraint allocates world_control_points and path_samples vectors inside the loop, and prepare_local_transform_buffers recomputes sin/cos for ALL bones per compute_world_transforms call. Fix: reuse buffers as persistent members, cache local transforms, use sincosf for batch trig.
acceptanceCriteria: solved_poses is a persistent member, cleared+resized instead of allocated each frame.
Path constraint reuses pre-allocated world_control_points and path_samples vectors.
Local transform sin/cos results are cached and only recomputed for modified bones.
Zero heap allocations in the constraint solving hot path (verified by allocator counter).
marrow_benchmark shows reduced constraint_us and overall frame time.

stories.title: Unify to single-precision float and eliminate AoS/SoA dual sync
stories.description: The system maintains two parallel transform representations: AoS (double, 48 bytes/bone) and SoA (float, 24 bytes/bone) with frequent sync copies. For 2D skeletal animation, float precision is sufficient. Unify to float SoA as the canonical representation, eliminating redundant sync passes and halving memory bandwidth.
acceptanceCriteria: BoneWorldTransform uses float instead of double for a, b, c, d, world_x, world_y.
Only one transform representation exists (no dual AoS/SoA sync).
All constraint solvers operate on the unified float representation.
Memory per bone drops from 72 bytes to 24 bytes (SoA float only).
All smoke tests pass within float precision tolerance.
marrow_benchmark shows improved cache performance and reduced sync overhead.

stories.title: Add indexed timeline lookup and cached keyframe cursor
stories.description: Research found animation_us bottleneck comes from: (1) find_rotate_timeline() etc. doing O(N) std::find_if per bone per timeline type — replace with O(1) bone_timeline_index array built at load time. (2) Keyframe search starts at index 0 every frame — add per-timeline cached last_keyframe_index in a SamplingContext (ozz-animation pattern). Spine v4 uses cached linear scan over binary search because for 2-20 keys, forward cache beats binary search.
acceptanceCriteria: AnimationData stores bone_timeline_index: flat array indexed by bone_index with pointers to each timeline type.
SamplingContext stores last_keyframe_index per active timeline per track, lives on Skeleton instance.
Forward playback starts search from cached index instead of 0.
Animation loop resets cache to 0.
marrow_benchmark animation_us drops by at least 40%.
All smoke tests produce identical results.

stories.title: Eliminate constant tracks and convert animation data to float32
stories.description: Research: (1) All keyframe data uses double (8 bytes) — for 2D animation float (4 bytes) provides identical visual quality and doubles cache density. (2) ozz-animation's AnimationOptimizer removes redundant keyframes where all keys match setup pose — typical Spine exports have 20-40% redundant timelines. (3) Replace solve_cubic_bezier_parameter Newton+bisection (40 iterations) with a pre-computed 64-entry LUT per unique bezier curve.
acceptanceCriteria: All keyframe times, values, and interpolation parameters use float instead of double.
Constant/identity timelines (single key matching setup pose) are pruned at load time.
Bezier curves use a 64-entry pre-computed LUT with linear interpolation instead of Newton solver.
Animation data memory reduced by ~40% (measured and logged).
marrow_benchmark animation_us drops by at least 30% additional.
All smoke tests pass within float precision tolerance.

stories.title: Move mesh skinning to GPU: static VBO + bone palette only
stories.description: Research found the vertex shader ALREADY does bone blending but the CPU ALSO pre-computes skinned vertices redundantly (evaluate_skinned_vertices in module.cpp, evaluate_current_mesh_attachment in skeleton_skin.cpp). Fix: store bind-pose bone-local vertex data as static vertex attributes (uploaded once), skip CPU skinning for non-clipped meshes, only upload 3KB bone palette per frame. Expected: 79μs → ~2μs per skeleton (96% reduction).
acceptanceCriteria: DynamicMeshDrawCommand vertex_payloads are built once at attachment creation, not per frame.
evaluate_skinned_vertices() is skipped for meshes without active clip masks.
Per-frame data upload is bone palette only (128 bones x 24 bytes = 3KB).
marrow_benchmark skinning_us drops from ~79μs to <5μs per skeleton.
Meshes with FFD deforms upload a secondary dynamic offset buffer only when offsets change.
All smoke tests produce identical visual results.

stories.title: Replace CPU polygon clipping with stencil-only clip path
stories.description: Research: apply_dynamic_mesh_mask_geometry does CPU Sutherland-Hodgman clipping to compute skinned vertex positions for clip intersection. This forces CPU skinning for clipped meshes. Replace with pure stencil-based clipping: render clip polygon to stencil, render skinned mesh with stencil test. Eliminates the last remaining CPU skinning dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: Clipped mesh attachments render via stencil test, not CPU polygon intersection.
apply_dynamic_mesh_mask_geometry is removed from the hot path.
CPU mesh skinning code is only used for SkeletonBounds hit testing (not rendering).
marrow_benchmark skinning_us is near zero even with clip attachments active.
Visual clipping results match the previous CPU path.

stories.title: Convert constraint solver to float and add fast atan2
stories.description: Research found ALL IK/transform constraint math uses double precision (50+ locals, 38 transcendentals) but output is stored as float — wasting 30-40% of constraint time. Also std::atan2(double) is 1.5-2x slower than atan2f(float). Fix: convert all constraint solver locals to float, replace 12 std::atan2 calls with polynomial approximation (0.005 rad / 0.28° max error, 6x faster), replace std::acos/sin/cos/sqrt with fast float equivalents.
acceptanceCriteria: All local variables in apply_one_bone_ik and apply_two_bone_ik are float.
All local variables in path and transform constraint solvers are float.
std::atan2 replaced with fast_atan2f polynomial (max error 0.005 rad).
std::acos/sin/cos replaced with fast float approximations.
marrow_benchmark constraint_us drops by at least 35%.
All constraint smoke tests pass within 0.3° tolerance.

stories.title: Add per-constraint dirty-flag skipping
stories.description: Research: constraint loop evaluates every IK/transform constraint unconditionally every frame. For idle/partially-animated skeletons, many constraints have unchanged inputs. Add per-constraint dirty detection: track whether input bones changed since last solve, skip if unchanged. Spine considers IK cheap but Marrow's 56μs suggests overhead.
acceptanceCriteria: Each constraint tracks revision numbers of its input bones (target + constrained).
If no input bone revision changed, the constraint evaluation is skipped entirely.
Skipped constraints produce identical output (validated by comparison test).
For idle skeletons (no animation playing), constraint_us drops to near zero.
For partially animated skeletons, only affected constraints re-evaluate.
marrow_benchmark shows per-constraint skip count in output.

stories.title: Validate performance: target 200 skeletons at 60fps
stories.description: After all optimization stories (MAR-087–098), run comprehensive benchmarks. Original profiling: animation 81μs + skinning 79μs + constraint 56μs + render 12μs + transform 4μs = ~232μs/skeleton. Targets after optimization: animation <30μs, skinning <5μs, constraint <25μs, render <12μs, transform <4μs = <76μs/skeleton → 219 skeletons at 60fps.
acceptanceCriteria: marrow_benchmark --skeletons 200 completes a frame in <16.6ms.
Benchmark score >= 80/100.
animation_us < 30μs per skeleton.
skinning_us < 5μs per skeleton.
constraint_us < 25μs per skeleton.
SIMD NEON path shows >1.5x speedup over scalar.
Results documented in AGENTS.md with before/after comparison table.

stories.title: Convert BoneTransform from double to float
stories.description: Performance investigation found BoneTransform's 7 double fields are the primary reason constraint_us didn't improve. Every IK/transform constraint does 7 double→float reads + 7 float→double writes per bone, negating the fast trig savings. BoneWorldTransform is already float. Convert BoneTransform to float — sufficient precision for 2D animation (sub-pixel accuracy at position 10000).
acceptanceCriteria: BoneTransform uses float for all 7 fields (x, y, rotation, scale_x, scale_y, shear_x, shear_y).
No double↔float casts remain in IK and transform constraint solvers.
AttachmentVertex converted to float.
All smoke tests pass within float precision tolerance.
marrow_benchmark constraint_us drops by at least 20%.

stories.title: Convert PhysicsBoneState and PathDistanceSample to float
stories.description: PhysicsBoneState has 14 double fields (112 bytes/bone), physics simulation runs entirely in double. PathDistanceSample uses double for distance and point coordinates. Both are unnecessary — world transforms are float. Convert to float to halve memory and eliminate precision boundary crossings.
acceptanceCriteria: PhysicsBoneState uses float for all 14 fields (56 bytes/bone, was 112).
PathDistanceSample and sample_path_distance use float.
No double↔float casts remain in physics and path constraint code.
Physics smoke tests pass within float tolerance.
marrow_benchmark constraint_us drops by an additional 10-15%.

stories.title: Replace ensure_all_world_transforms_current with targeted subtree update
stories.description: After IK/transform constraints, ensure_all_world_transforms_current (line 1535) scans ALL 64 bones checking revision flags. Only 6-8 bones were actually modified by 11 constraints. Replace with a targeted approach: collect modified bone indices during constraint solving, then only recompute world transforms for those bones and their children.
acceptanceCriteria: Constraint solving collects a dirty bone set (indices of modified bones).
After all constraints, only dirty bones and their descendants get world transform updates.
No full linear scan of all bones occurs post-constraint.
World transforms are identical to the previous full-scan approach.
marrow_benchmark constraint_us drops by an additional 5-10%.

stories.title: Fix double literal promotions in constraint math
stories.description: Line 1332 in skeleton_constraints.cpp uses 4.0 (double literal) instead of 4.0f, promoting the entire discriminant subexpression to double. Audit all numeric literals in constraint code and ensure f suffix on all float constants to prevent implicit double promotions.
acceptanceCriteria: All numeric literals in constraint solver use f suffix (4.0f, 0.5f, etc.).
No implicit double promotions remain (verified by -Wdouble-promotion compiler warning).
Constraint results unchanged.
Build with -Wdouble-promotion produces zero warnings in skeleton_constraints.cpp.

stories.title: Validate constraint performance: target <25μs
stories.description: After MAR-100–103, validate that constraint_us meets the <25μs target. Expected: BoneTransform float (20%↓) + PhysicsBoneState float (10-15%↓) + targeted subtree (5-10%↓) + literal fix (1-2%↓) = 36-47% total reduction. 59μs * 0.55 ≈ 32μs. May need additional optimization if not met.
acceptanceCriteria: marrow_benchmark --frames 240 --samples 5 shows constraint_us < 35μs.
Overall score >= 80/100.
max_skeletons_60fps >= 180.
Results documented in AGENTS.md with full before/after table.
All smoke and unit tests pass.

stories.title: Fix editor shell float/double drift from animation scalar conversion
stories.description: The Phase 5 animation float32 conversion (MAR-094) changed AnimationScalar from double to float, but src/editor/shell_main.cpp still uses double* in bezier editing lambdas and likely other locations. This causes 9 compile errors blocking the editor build. Audit the entire editor shell for double→float type mismatches and fix all occurrences so the editor compiles and runs.
acceptanceCriteria: cmake --build build --target marrow_editor_shell compiles with zero errors.
All editor bezier/keyframe editing lambdas use float* (AnimationScalar*) instead of double*.
No double→float warnings remain in shell_main.cpp with -Wdouble-promotion.
marrow_project_smoke passes.
The editor shell launches and opens without crash.

stories.title: Fix renderer_sample setup-pose region attachment placement check
stories.description: Ralph error log shows marrow_renderer_sample --skip-render fails on an inherited setup-pose region-attachment placement assertion. The float conversion changed bone transform precision, causing the hard-coded expected values in the renderer sample to drift. Update the expected values to match float-precision world transforms.
acceptanceCriteria: marrow_renderer_sample --skip-render assets/fixtures/player_idle.mskl assets/fixtures/player_idle.matl passes.
marrow_renderer_sample --hud --skip-render passes.
Setup-pose expected quad coordinates match float-precision bone world transforms.
All other renderer_sample validation paths pass.

stories.title: Fix fixture smoke IK world-transform pass count assertion
stories.description: Ralph error log shows marrow_fixture_smoke fails on 'IK constraint solve should perform exactly one full world-transform pass per update' assertion. The constraint subtree optimization changed the world-transform update pattern. Update the assertion to match the new targeted-subtree approach.
acceptanceCriteria: marrow_fixture_smoke assets/fixtures/player_idle.mskl assets/fixtures/player_idle.matl passes.
IK constraint validation assertions reflect the targeted-subtree world-transform pattern.
All other fixture smoke validations pass unchanged.

stories.title: Full build validation: all targets compile and all tests pass
stories.description: After MAR-105–107, verify the entire project builds cleanly and all test/smoke/benchmark targets pass. This is the gate for declaring Phase 5+6 optimization complete.
acceptanceCriteria: cmake --build build completes with zero errors (ALL targets including editor_shell).
marrow_fixture_smoke passes (both JSON and binary paths).
marrow_unit_tests passes.
marrow_project_smoke passes.
marrow_renderer_sample --skip-render passes.
marrow_editor_shell launches without crash.
build-bench/marrow_benchmark --skeletons 200 reports score >= 80.
AGENTS.md validation table is up to date.
